revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[arz:اينويا جو]][[en:Iinoya-gū]][[fr:Iinoya-gū]][[ja:井伊谷宮]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Kita-ku, Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Iinoya-gū
井伊谷宮 | image = File:Iinoya-gū.jpg | caption = Iinoya-gū in Hamamatsu, Shizuoka. | map_type = Japan Shizuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.8292|N|137.668|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Prince {{ill|Prince Munenaga|en|Prince Munenaga|qid=Q541057|lt=Prince Munenaga|1=Prince Munenaga}} | founded_by = {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|qid=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan|1=Government of Meiji Japan}} | established = 1872 | date_destroyed = | location = {{ill|Kita-ku, Hamamatsu|en|Kita-ku, Hamamatsu|qid=Q1131477|lt=Kita-ku, Hamamatsu|1=Kita-ku, Hamamatsu}} {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, Japan | website = {{URL|http://iinoyaguu.ehoh.net}} | architecture_style = | festival = September 22 | leadership = | location = {{ill|Hamamatsu|qid=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}; {{ill|Hamana-ku|qid=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}} }} {{nihongo|'''Iinoya-gū'''|井伊谷宮|Iinoya-gū}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Kita-ku, Hamamatsu|en|Kita-ku, Hamamatsu|qid=Q1131477|lt=Kita-ku|1=Kita-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|qid=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It was established in 1872, and its main festival is held annually on September 22. It is one of the [[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]]. ==History== Iinoya-gū is dedicated to the [[kami|deified spirit]] of Prince {{ill|Prince Munenaga|en|Prince Munenaga|qid=Q541057|lt=Prince Munenaga|1=Prince Munenaga}}, the fourth son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|qid=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, who died on this location in 1385. Munenaga was appointed as {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|qid=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}} by his father, and fought on behalf of the [[Southern Court]] against {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|qid=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}. Long after the establishment of the [[:en:Muromachi shogunate|Muromachi shogunate]] and Munenaga refused to accept defeat and continued his resistance in the mountains of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|qid=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi|1=Tōtōmi Province}} and {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|qid=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}s until his death. Following the [[Meiji Restoration|Meiji restoration]], the new {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|qid=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan|1=Government of Meiji Japan}} wished to propagate a sense of loyalty to the [[:en:Imperial family of Japan|Imperial family of Japan]] and to emphasize the legitimacy of the Southern Court. The former ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Hikone Domain|en|Hikone Domain|qid=Q1062225|lt=Hikone Domain|1=Hikone Domain}}, {{ill|Ii Naonori|en|Ii Naonori|qid=Q7270239|lt=Ii Naonori|1=Ii Naonori}} brought the site of Prince Munenaga's grave to the attention of the authorities. The grave was located in Ii-no-ya valley, which also happened to be the ancestral home of the {{ill|Ii clan|en|Ii clan|qid=Q2243336|lt=Ii clan|1=Ii clan}}. Construction on a shrine began in 1870 and the building was completed in 1872. In 1873, the shrine was given the Rank of (官幣中社, ''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-chūsha]]''), or imperial shrine of the second Rank, in the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] under {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}}. ==See also== *[[List of the Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration|Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] *[[:en:Kamakura-gu|Kamakura-gu]] ==External links== {{commons Category-inline|Iinoya-gū}} *[http://iinoyaguu.ehoh.net Official website] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration}} [[en:Iinoya-gū]] [[arz:اينويا جو]] {{Commons category|Iinoya-gū}} [[fr:Iinoya-gū]] [[ja:井伊谷宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11373321}} [[Category:1872 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hamamatsu]] [[Category:Fifteen Shrines of the Kenmu Restoration]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
